channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Belarus strongman set to win a 7th term in an election the opposition calls a farce
item.description: The smiling face of President Alexander Lukashenko gazed out from campaign posters across Belarus on Sunday as the country held an orchestrated election virtually guaranteed to give the 70-year-old autocrat yet another term on top of his three decades in power.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Ceasefires in Gaza and Lebanon appear at risk with disputes over deadlines
item.description: Ceasefires in Gaza and Lebanon appeared at risk on Sunday as disputes continued over key deadlines, and local health officials said that Israeli forces opened fire on both fronts. In Gaza, Israel accused Hamas of violating the ceasefire there by changing the order of hostages it has released.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why they are likely to refuse
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip is likely to be met with a hard “no" from the two U.S. allies and the Palestinians themselves who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Thousands in Ireland still without power after Storm Éowyn, as more wild weather wallops UK, France
item.description: Ireland has called in help from England and France to restore power to hundreds of thousands of people after the most disruptive storm for years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on the thousands disappeared in Colombia, Peru and Paraguay
item.description: Thousands of people have disappeared in Latin America during decades-long conflicts. Many have never been found, presumed to be the victims of dictatorships, insurgencies or organized crime.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Peru's disappeared: Dozens look for relatives lost to violence. A woman who knows their sorrow helps
item.description: AYACUCHO, Perú (AP) — The easiest thing might have been to let go. To refresh the flowers at her husband’s grave and find comfort in retrieving his bones, a milestone in a country where 20,000 people disappeared between 1980 and 2000.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's world leaders club: who’s in and who's looking to salvage ties
item.description: President Donald Trump's second term is already bringing massive changes as global leaders, allies and adversaries alike, watch to see where they fall in the pecking order and vie for a seat at the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: International peacekeepers killed as fighting rages around eastern Congo's key city
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Fighting with M23 rebels in eastern Congo has left at least 13 peacekeepers and foreign soldiers dead, United Nations and army officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump wants Jordan and Egypt to accept more Palestinian refugees and floats plan to 'clean out' Gaza
item.description: President Donald Trump says he would like to see Arab nations increase the number of Palestinian refugees they are accepting from the Gaza Strip, potentially moving out enough of the population to “clean out” the area to create a virtual clean slate.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Devastating toll for Gaza's children: Over 13,000 killed and an estimated 25,000 injured, UN says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The war in Gaza has been devastating for children: More than 13,000 have been killed, an estimated 25,000 injured, and at least 25,000 hospitalized for malnutrition, according to U.N. agencies. As Britain’s deputy U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN refugee chief calls for more help for Syria as refugees begin to return en masse
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The United Nations refugee chief said Saturday that some 200,000 refugees have returned to Syria from neighboring countries since the government of Bashar Assad was overthrown last month.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli military sets up roadblocks in southern Lebanon and announces it won’t withdraw by deadline
item.description: MAYS AL-JABAL, Lebanon (AP) — Israel's military Saturday set up roadblocks across border towns and roads in a strategic valley in southern Lebanon, a day before the deadline for it to withdraw from the area under an agreement that halted its war with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Koreans turn funeral wreaths and K-pop light sticks into political protest tools
item.description: SEOUL (AP) — South Koreans are repurposing flower wreaths and K-pop light sticks as political protest tools amid the nation’s deepest political crisis in decades, sparked by President Yoon Suk Yeol’s short-lived martial law declaration in December.
item.dc:creator: JUWON PARK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auschwitz 80 years since liberation: Ryszard Horowitz's story of survival and making the American Dream
item.description: An exclusive interview with Auschwitz survivor and award-winning photographer Ryszard Horowitz, on his extraordinary life and his perilous childhood as a Jewish boy in Nazi-occupied Kraków.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fighting in Sudan's civil war sets ablaze the country's largest oil refinery, satellite photos show
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Fighting around Sudan 's largest oil refinery set the sprawling complex ablaze, satellite data analyzed by The Associated Press on Saturday shows, sending thick, black smoke over the country's capital. Forces loyal to Sudan’s military under army chief Gen.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases 4 Israeli women in latest ceasefire move
item.description: Palestinian militant group Hamas has released four Israeli female soldiers who were held in the Gaza Strip as part of the latest ceasefire agreement, according to the Israeli military. In return, the Jewish State released 70 Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Hamas releases 4 female hostages as part of Israel ceasefire deal
item.description: The terrorist group Hamas released four more hostages from Gaza on Saturday under a ceasefire deal with Israel that took effect nearly a week ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Philippines accuses China's forces of harassing fisheries vessels in the South China Sea
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Chinese coast guard ships and a Chinese navy helicopter harassed a group of Philippine fisheries vessels conducting a scientific survey in a hotly disputed area of the South China Sea, forcing them to cancel the operation, the Philippine coast guard said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Desperate families await return of 4 female soldiers held by Hamas for 15 months
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas announced Friday that it would release four female soldiers held hostage for 15 months in Gaza, as part of an exchange for dozens of Palestinian prisoners laid out in its ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SHLOMO MOR and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump had ‘fiery’ call with Danish prime minister over Greenland: Report
item.description: President Trump reportedly held a “fiery” call with Denmark’s Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen over the president’s insistence that U.S. control of Greenland is necessary for American national security.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hamas releases names of 4 female hostages to be released next, possibly violates deal
item.description: Hamas says it will release four female IDF soldiers including Karina Ariev, Daniella Gilboa, Naama Levy and Liri Albag in second hostage release in a possible violation of the ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel’s military will remain in Lebanon as ceasefire deadline nears
item.description: Israel said its forces will remain in southern Lebanon after UN and Lebanese forces moved too slowly under the truce with Hezbollah established in November, which remains unfulfilled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu suggests Israel might not complete its withdrawal from Lebanon by a ceasefire deadline
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu suggested Friday that Israel might not withdraw all of its forces from Lebanon by a deadline set in its ceasefire with Hezbollah, and Washington appears prepared to push for an extension.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin echoes Trump's claim that conflict in Ukraine could have been avoided had he been in office
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday echoed U.S. President Donald Trump's claim the conflict in Ukraine could have been prevented had he been in the White House in 2022. He also said Moscow is ready for talks with the U.S. on a broad range of issues.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS greenlights swift deportation for migrants Biden allowed in temporarily
item.description: The acting head of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is allowing immigration enforcement agents to swiftly deport those who came to the U.S. under multiple pathways established under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Trump: Zelensky 'shouldn't have allowed' war with Russia to happen
item.description: President Trump suggested Friday that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky bore some responsibility for the war that began almost three years ago with Russia's invasion. “Zelensky — I will say this, he wants to settle now. He's had enough.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump: Make products in America or face tariffs
item.description: “Come make your product in America, and we will give you among the lowest taxes of any nation on earth,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Putin reportedly concerned over Russia's economy ahead of possible Trump tariffs
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is reportedly worried about the state of the country's economy amid threat of tariffs from the U.S. as President Donald Trump returns to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mother of missing Marine veteran calls Trump admin a 'breath of fresh air' as she continues 12-year search
item.description: Debra Tice, mother of missing Marine veteran and American journalist Austin Tice, has returned to Syria to search for her son who was taken captive in August 2012.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tabloids apologize to Prince Harry, admit intruding on Princess Diana
item.description: Prince Harry claimed a monumental victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House cheers release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden, Qatar
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JON GAMBRELL and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation
